name: Viana de Bolo – return via Alto de San Bernabé
name_original: Viana de Bolo – vuelta por Alto de San Bernabé
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Viana Do Bolo are set within the rugged southeastern mountains of Ourense, Galicia, Spain. The region features significant topographical complexity, with high mountain ranges bordering deep river valleys carved by the Bibei, Camba, and O Vao rivers. This diverse terrain, including ancient forests and reservoirs like Embalse de O Bao, provides varied and challenging environments for mountain biking. Glacial lakes at higher altitudes also contribute to the unique natural landscape. Best mountain bike trails around Viana Do Bolo The most popular mountain bike trail is Viana de Bolo – return via Alto de San Bernabé, a 21.5 miles (34.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers significant elevation changes through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sanctuary of La Tuiza – Lubián Village loop from Lubián, a difficult 51.3 miles (82.5 km) path. It features challenging climbs and descents with extensive views of the surrounding valleys. Local mountain bikers also love the O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge – Pazo de Arnado loop from O Cruceiro, a 44.6 miles (71.7 km) trail leading through river valleys and forested areas, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Viana Do Bolo is defined by high mountains, deep river valleys, and ancient forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Viana Do Bolo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 1.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 59 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Viana Do Bolo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Komoot currently lists 8 mountain bike trails around Viana Do Bolo. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate to difficult, traversing the region's rugged mountains and deep river valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The terrain in Viana Do Bolo is characterized by significant topographical complexity. You can expect a mix of high mountain ranges, deep river valleys carved by the Bibei, Camba, and O Vao rivers, and ancient forests. Routes often feature challenging climbs and descents, with paths winding through wooded areas and along reservoirs.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for different skill levels in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Yes, the trails cater to various skill levels, though most are geared towards experienced riders. There are 5 moderate routes and 3 difficult routes available. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994317938/viana-de-bolo-return-via-alto-de-san-bernabe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viana de Bolo – return via Alto de San Bernabé&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza – Lubián Village loop from Lubián&lt;/a&gt; offers a more difficult challenge.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Viana Do Bolo are designed as loops. For instance, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14936772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Bañadoiro Suspension Bridge – Pazo de Arnado loop from O Cruceiro&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through river valleys and forested areas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza – Lubián Village loop from Lubián&lt;/a&gt;, offering extensive views.

Q: What natural features can I see along mountain bike trails in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter impressive centenarian forests, crystalline rivers, and streams. The landscape is shaped by the Bibei, Camba, and O Vao rivers, with prominent reservoirs like Embalse de O Bao. Higher up, you might even find glacial lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de Carrizais&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or lakes to visit while mountain biking?
A: Yes, the area features several beautiful water bodies. You can find glacial lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de Carrizais&lt;/a&gt; at higher altitudes, offering stunning views. There's also a notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;waterfall at Embalse de Valdesirgas&lt;/a&gt;, which could be a rewarding stop on your ride.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints along the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely. Viana Do Bolo is known for its panoramic vistas. You can find viewpoints offering breathtaking scenes of the reservoirs and surrounding mountains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7989709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Puente Porto reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Beo Reservoir and Viana de Bolo from the San Cibrao viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. These make for excellent breaks and photo opportunities.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on Viana Do Bolo mountain bike trails?
A: Given the region's mountainous character, expect significant elevation changes. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Mezquita loop from A Gudiña-Porta de Galicia&lt;/a&gt; involves nearly 909 meters of ascent, while the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Serro – A Portela loop from As Ermidas&lt;/a&gt; has over 832 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Viana Do Bolo?
A: While many trails in Viana Do Bolo are moderate to difficult, some hiking and walking routes in the region, like the Senda Verde do Xares around Embalse de Prada, are suitable for both walking and cycling and might offer gentler options for families. It's always best to check the specific route details for suitability before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The komoot community appreciates the diverse and challenging environment for mountain biking in Viana Do Bolo. Riders often highlight the dramatic landscapes, including high mountains, deep river valleys, and ancient forests, which provide a constantly engaging and scenic experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Viana Do Bolo?
A: Many outdoor trails in Galicia are dog-friendly, especially in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas like the Parque Natural de O Invernadeiro, which is nearby. Keep your dog under control and be mindful of wildlife and other trail users.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Viana Do Bolo?
A: The diverse climate of Galicia means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winters can bring colder weather and potential snow at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before your ride.
tips: When you reach the top of Alto de Campo, you are rewarded with a spectacular view of the Embalse do Bao. don't forget to look back

Viana do Bolo is located in the set of depressions in the southeastern Sierra of Galicia, Pena Trevinca. Built on the ruins of a small fort that is believed to have been first settled when a gold mine was discovered before the Roman Empire settled in the area. The town is located on the banks of the O Vao reservoir, on the Bibei river
